The first screen in my $44k/month app onboarding: 

'Congratulations'

That's it.

It reaffirms their decision before they even start.

This one screen sets the tone for everything.

My onboarding makes users face their own problems.

I ask questions that make them want to fix it.

My conversion rate was trash until I installed a hard paywall.

· 2% conversion rate

After: Hard paywall (users had to commit to free trial)

What people don’t understand about GLP-1s is this:

The “appetite suppression” some people feel on them…

might just be how you naturally feel.

Hunger isn’t equal across humans.

Different leptin sensitivity.
Different insulin sensitivity.
Different GLP receptor response.

The real goal of glp agonists should be to simply to remove mental bandwidth and friction adhering to a calorie deficit.

That is it.

12 cals per pound of goal body weight. 0.8g of protein per pound of goal body weight.

Lift 2-3 days per week. 10k+ steps daily. 

Minimum

A strong use case for glp agonists is not weight loss.

It’s removing mental bandwidth in getting lean and staying lean.

I am first to admit how hard it is to really nail the deficit and get very lean.

text: my therapist dropped a truth bomb on me: “high functioning depression is scary as hell because you’re very capable of hiding emotions, feelings and thoughts, so no one knows you're not okay and even if you say something no one realises the severity, because you don’t seem like

top_replies.text:
Depression, at it's core, is a feedback loop:

• In the head, relentless negative self-talk
• In the heart, stuck anger (sometimes grief)
• In the nervous system: chronic anxiety and adrenal exhaustion.

And so the solution is to interrupt the loop.

You do this by questioning

Omg you just described me to a T. 
I'm so sorry you went through so much hell
Smiling through the storm while drowning inside. More people relate to this than you know.

The hardest part is when functioning well becomes your own trap. 

The better you hold it together, the less anyone sees there’s nothing left to hold with.
It gets worse with age. Oh wait no. Let's CBT that.. We're still alive.

I know so many people that just have their own problems. I refuse to add on to that. Because my problems always feel petty in comparison. Even when they're not. I'd rather just fade away than add to the misery. 

It's quite lonely. But it works for me.
I've never seen a more accurate description of my mental state before. wow. although I don't know if I would call myself "high functioning". I think maybe "medium functioning" is more like it.

Or worse, you know that you can technically get something done so you feel lazy if you don't do it. But by doing it it takes a huge toll on your mental health and makes you even worse. But you can't allow yourself to take the rest you need, because you could technically do it.
Being “high functioning” can feel like a superpower and a curse at the same time. You’re handling life on the surface, but underneath you’re quietly carrying weight most people don’t even notice.
That hit hard. High-functioning depression is invisible people see competence but not the chaos underneath. Speaking up is brave, even if it’s hard for others to fully grasp.

It begins with vision.

Visualise the person you want to become and reverse engineer the exact steps you need to take to reach that identity.

What are the daily habits?
What does he work on?
How does he move, act and behave?

Ingrain these into your life...

And you'll slowly

Personality isn't some fixed biological trait, it’s literally just a collection of habits. If you start acting like the person you want to be, your brain eventually stops pretending and just becomes that person.
Don’t act successful. Install successful habits.

YOU ARE LITERALLY A BAG OF MEAT AND BONE WITH A CONSCIOUSNESS.

You decide whether that conscious experience is enjoyable or miserable via the mental attitude/ frequency/ identity you approach life and yourself with.

Therefore, happiness and success are a choice.
faith it til you make ittt

But every identity started as a decision.

Athletes acted disciplined before it felt natural.
Leaders acted confident before they believed it.
Calm people practiced calm before they became it.

You don’t “find” yourself.
You rehearse yourself.

So act like
